title: triticum aegilopoides var. larionowii
intro: Triticum aegilopoides var. larionowii, also known as Larionov's wild wheat or Larionov's spelt, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the wild wheat (Triticum aegilopoides) native to Eastern Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health issues. Triticum aegilopoides var. larionowii can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its seeds can be used as a nutritious food supplement.
